def: s.m. Brain; — marrow; — kernel; pith; pulp; crumb (of bread); the inside (of anything), the chief substance or essence (of anything); — intellect; — pride, arrogance; — the best kind of pearl
more: n. m. See dimāg . 1. The brain; encephalon ( bhejā ). 2. The inside; marrow; kernel; pith. 3. The crumb (breas). 4. The best kind of pearl. 5. Intellect. 6. Pride. magz uṛnā , v. n. To feel distracted. magz bhauṅkānā, phirānā, khālī karnā, yā mārnā ; Ped. magz-zanī k. , v. n. To fatigue oneself with talking. magz pachchī k. , v. a. To rack one's brains. magz chalnā , v. n. To be turned (one's head). magz se utārnā, yā nikālnā , v. n. To make out of one's own head; to invent. magz-kā , adj. Cerebral; pertaining to the brain. magz ko chaṛhnā , v. n. To get to one's head. magz khānā, yā chāṭnā , v. a. To tease; worry; harrass. magz ke kīṛe jhaṛnā, magz kī kīl nikalnā , v. n. 1. To receive castigation. 2. To be humbled. magz kī nikalnā , ( Slang ) v. n. 1. To be emptied (of semen). 2. To be exhausted. be-magz, be-magzā , adj. 1. Without kernel; empty. 2. Brainless; empty-headed.

s. m. 1. The brain, marrow, kernel, pith. 2. adj. Of great energy, or vigour; very vigorous, or very energetic; — of great splendour; full of fire; very bright
s.m. A great tīrth or place of pilgrimage
adj. (f. -ā), Exceedingly sharp (as a weapon, or perception, &c.); very pungent (as a flavour); — s.m. A very acute person
or (dialec.) mahā-jālā, s.m. A large fishing-net, a seine
adj. & s.m. Having a great jaṭā, q.v.; — an epithet of Śiva
s.f. A great braid or coil of hair; the matted or twisted hair of Śiva
or mahā-jaṛ, adj. Utterly irrational; intensely stupid, or insensible
s.m. (f. -ī), A great or eminent man, a very distinguished person; a good or trustworthy man; a man of credit; a banker, money-dealer; a merchant, a wholesale dealer

s.f. Great risk, or peril; a great enterprise; a great achievement
s.m. (f. -dātri), A great giver, a very beneficent person

adj. (f. -ā), Exceedingly vile, exceptionally bad; — s.m. An exceedingly vile person, a great miscreant
s.m. A large tooth, or tusk; an elephant's tusk; — adj. & s.m. Having large teeth, or tusks; — an elephant with large tusks
s.m. 'The great metal, or element'; gold
vulg. mahā-deʼo, s.m. 'The great deity,' an epithet of Śiva (the third deity of the Hindū triad; it is the name by which he is most commonly known)

s.m. A large and splendid sort of sedan (or pālkī) for great personages
s.f. = mahā-rātri; and corr. of mahā-rāshṭrī, qq.v.
s.m., or mahā-rātri, s.f. Midnight, the dead of night; late at night; time after midnight; close of night
s.f. The longest night in the year; the great night of the complete destruction of the world; — the eighth day (or night) in the light half of the month Aśvin

s.m. A great king; reigning prince, supreme sovereign, paramount power (i.q. mahā-rājā); — a form of address to a Brāhmaṇ, or a superior; your Majesty! your Excellency! your Honour! Sir! — mahā-rājā, s.m. Supreme power, sovereign, emperor
(˚ja+adh˚), s.m. Lord paramount, king of kings, universal emperor
s.m. The rank or title of a reigning sovereign; sovereignty, dominion
s.m. A great kingdom or realm; 'the great country,' i.e. the Marhaṭṭā country; — name of a kind of metre in Sanskrit prosody
s.m. pl. The Mahraṭṭā people, the Mahraṭṭās
or mahā-rāshṭī, s.f. The Mahraṭṭī language; — s.m. A Marhaṭṭā Brāhmaṇ
s.m. The great Rāmāyan, one of the many Rāmāyaṇs of Vālmīki
or mahā-rānī, s.f. A great queen; the principal wife of a Rājā; a queen in her own right, reigning queen; empress; — a respectful form of address to Hindū ladies of rank
s.m. (f. -nī, or inī), A great traveller
s.m. A large car, or vehicle; a great chariot; — one who has a great chariot, a great warrior or hero

vulg. mahā-sāntpan, s.m. lit. 'Great tormenting'; a kind of severe penance (viz. subsisting for six successive days respectively on cow's urine, cow-dung, milk, curds, ghī, and water in which Kuśa-grass has been boiled, and fasting on the seventh day; or, instead of one day, some authorities assign a period of three days to each penance, considering the first kind as the common sāntpan; others omit the sixth and seventh penance, making the whole last fifteen days)
s.m. Excessive violence, great cruelty or outrage, brutal assault; — extreme daring or audacity
s.f. A great, or royal, council; a senate
adj. & s.m. Having a great or noble essence; noble, good, virtuous, just; — a noble being; a virtuous person
s.m. Great pleasure, excessive enjoyment; — copulation, coition
s.f. A very lovely woman, a great beauty
s.m. The skull; the temporal or frontal bone, the forehead; — the highest or last number of numeration (i.q. mahā-śankh)
adj. (f. -ā), Very fine, or minute; very subtile; very delicate
(˚hā+ash˚), s.f. 'The great eighth,' the eighth day of the light half of the month Asin (or festival in honour of Durgā, called the Durgā-pūjā)
s.m. A particular high number, one thousand billions, or a thousand millions; — one of Kuver's treasures; — the temporal or frontal bone, the forehead

(˚hā+āś˚), adj. & s.m. Having a noble disposition, high-minded, magnanimous, liberal, munificent; open, unsuspicious; — a noble-minded man; a respectable person, a gentleman; — a term of respectful address; your Honour! Sir! Master! — mahā-kāl, s.m. A form of Śiva; an epithet of Śiva in his character of the destroying deity (he being then represented of a black colour, and of more or less terrific aspect
s.f. A form, or an epithet, of Durgā (wife of Śiva), in her terrific form
adj. (f. -ā) 'Large-bodied,' of great stature, tall, large, gigantic; bulky, stout
s.m. A great work; — adj. Accomplishing great works; doing mighty deeds; — an epithet of Śiva
or mahā-kūl, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Of a great family, sprung from a noble family, high-born, noble, aristocratic; — a man of noble or good family, a noble
s.m. Name of a very large esculent root; — a sort of yam; — garlic
s.m. A large or deep well
s.m. 'Great cutaneous eruption'; the worst form of leprosy
s.m. A great bay, or creek; — inroad of the sea upon the adjoining low ground
s.m. 'The great division (of the earth),' an epithet of Bhārat-varsh
s.m. 'Great antidote,' a kind of medicinal compound; — great sickness, severe illness; fever; — adj. Armed with a great club
(˚hā+āl˚), s.m. A great dwelling; a great temple, or monastery; a place of refuge, sanctuary, asylum; — the great refuge, the Supreme Being, or great Universal Spirit
s.m. 'Great iron'; a magnet, a loadstone

or mahā-māri, or mahā-mārī, s.f. Great pestilence, or mortality; great slaughter or havoc; plague; epidemic disease; cholera-morbus; — mahā-mārī, s.f. 'The great destroying goddess,' an epithet of Durgā
s.m. A great pestilence; a great battle; great slaughter (i.q. mahā-māri); — a great fighter; a hard hitter
s.m. A kind of large bean, Dolichos catsang
s.m. 'Costly meat'; human flesh (one who takes money on giving his daughter in marriage is said to sell mahā-māṅs or 'human flesh')
s.f. One of the seven sisters who are goddesses of small-pox, &c.

s.f. Great deceit, or illusion; worldly illusion; the divine power of illusion (which makes the material universe appear as if really existing, and renders it cognizable by the senses); the Great Illusion (the illusory nature of worldly objects personified); an epithet of the goddess Durgā
s.m. & f. Costly gem; precious jewel (as the diamond, ruby, &c.)
s.m., or mahā-murkhatā, s.f. Exceeding folly
adj. (f. -ā), High-priced; very costly, or precious, or valuable; — s.m. A ruby
s.m. Great confusion or infatuation of mind; great illusion; great ignorance
or mahā-mahimā, adj. & s.m. Very, or extremely, great; truly great; high and mighty; — excessive greatness, omnipotence; true greatness
s.m. The coral-tree, Erythrina indica; — a species of medicinal plant; a particular drug (classed among the principal drugs, and described as a tonic and stimulant)
(˚hā+anu˚), adj. (f. -ā) & s.m. In high esteem, worthy, mighty, exalted, dignified, pre-eminent, just, virtuous; — a gentleman
s.m. Name of one of the twenty-one hells or places of torment
s.f. Midnight; the dead of night, the time after midnight
s.m. A great city; — name of a particular city
s.m. A species of large nimb-tree, or Melia sempervirens
(˚hā+ān˚), s.m. Great bliss, the great joy of deliverance from further transmigration; final emancipation, or beatitude; — a proper name
(fr. mahānand, q.v.), adj. Relating to Mahānand; — relating to final emancipation; — s.m. One who has attained final beatitude
vulg. mahā-naʼumī, or mahā-naumī, s.f. The ninth day in the light half of the month Asin; the festival held on that day
s.m. (lit. 'dark, or deep, blue') A kind of sapphire; — name of one of the Nāgas

s.m. Any long continuous composition or literary work, a magnum opus; — principal sentence, great proposition; epithet of twelve mystical utterances of the Upanishads (esp. of the mystic words Tattvam, and Om)
s.m. Deep thought, or consideration; profound meditation
s.m. The Gangetic porpoise, Delphinus gangeticus
adj. & s.m. Very poisonous or venomous; — the Coluber Nāga, or a kind of small and very venomous serpent (said to have two heads)
(for mahā-vishuv-sankrānt), s.f. The vernal equinox, the moment of the sun's passing into Aries (differing by several days from European computation)
s.m. A great wood; a large, or dense, forest
s.m. Name of one of the twenty-one hells or places of torment

s.m. A great hero; — a lion; — an epithet of Vishṇu, and of Garuḍ (the bird and vehicle of Vishṇu); — fire, sacrificial fire; a sacrificial vessel; — the thunder-bolt of Indra; — a white horse; — name of the last or twenty-fourth Arhat (the last and most celebrated Jain teacher of the present age; supposed to have flourished in the province of Behār in the sixth century before the Christian era)
adj. (f. -ā) & s.m. Very energetic; very powerful, or mighty; — a name of Brahmā; — a Brāhmaṇ
s.m. Great laughter; loud laughter; a horse-laugh

vulg. mahā-yagya, s.m. A great sacrifice or offering; a principal act of devotion, a sacrament (of the Hindū religion: of these acts five are enumerated; and they are severally considered as due to, 1˚ the Vedas; 2˚ the gods; 3˚ man; 4˚ the manes; 5˚ all created beings. The acts themselves are, 1˚ the study of Scripture; 2˚ the offering sacrifice to the gods; 3˚ hospitality to guests; 4˚ libations of water to the manes of deceased ancestors; 5˚ the casting of food on the ground, or into water, as an offering to created beings generally)
adj. Very glorious, very famous or illustrious, renowned, celebrated
vulg. mahā-jug, s.m. A great yug, a yug of the gods, the aggregate of the four yugs, Krita, Tretā, Dvāpar, and Kali ( = 4,320,000,000 years of mortals)
vulg. mahā-jantr, s.m. A great machine; a great mechanical work (as a lock, a dike, &c.).

def: s.f. A cow.

adj. ( In comp .) 1. Great; illustrious. 2. Very; extremely. 3. Grievous. mahā aprādh, yā dosh , n. m. A heinous sin or crime. mahā-aprādhī, mahā-pāpī , n. m. A great sinner or criminal. mahā ūt , n. m. A great fool. mahā-brāhman , n. m. See achāraj , 3. mahā-balī , adj. Very strong or powerful. mahā-bhārat , n. m. 1. A great war. 2. A grand epic poem by Vyāsadeva , containing an account of the dissensions and civil wars of the Kurus and Pāṅḍūs . mahā-prasād , n. m. The food or meat offered to a deity, esp. to Jagannāth. mahā-purush , n. m. 1. A holy personage. 2. ( Ironic .) A wicked person. mahā pūrā , n. m. A consummate knave. mahā pirlaë , n. f. Commercial usage. mahā-janī sākh , n. f. Mercantile or commercial credit. mahā-janī lekhā , n. m. Mercantile accounts. mahā-deo , n. m. A name of Shiva, one of the Hindū triad. mahā-ḍol , n. m. The great night of the complete destruction of the world. mahā-rāj, mahā-rājah, mahā-rājā adhirāj ; Rus. mhārāj , n. m. 1. A supreme or sovereign prince; a rājā of rājas ; the paramount power. 2. def: s.m. = P مہابت mahābat, s.f. (for A. مہابة, inf. n. of هاب (for هيب) 'to be afraid' (of), &c.), Fear, dread; awe; reverence; gravity; majesty, greatness, authority.
(from هيب) s. f. Majesty, greatness, reverence, fear, dread, awe.
s.f. = P مہابه mahāba, s.m. (for A. مہابة, inf. n. of هاب (for هيب) 'to be afraid' (of), &c.), Fear, dread; awe; reverence; gravity; majesty, greatness, authority.

ety: n. of place fr. هبّ 'to blow with violence,' &c.
def: s.m. A breathing-hole, a vent; a place whence, or whither, the wind blows.
(noun of place from هبّ) s. m. 1. A vent. 2. A place whence or whither the wind blows. ¶ muhibb, part. act. (IV.) 1. Waking, rousing. 2. Libidine actus et in fœmellam insiliens admissarius.

ety: for A. محبة, v.n.fr. حبّ 'to be loved,' &c.
def: s.f. Love, affection; friendship (syn. dostī)
more: n. f. 1. Love; affection ( piyār , 1). 1. Muṅh dekhe kī muhabbat hai . Prov. Friendship before one's face. 2. Lene dene ke muṅh meṅ khāk, mahabbat baṛī chīz hai . Prov. Saying. Giving and taking are mean, there's nothing like pure friendship. (The miser's maxim). 2. Friendship ( dostī ). muhabbat-āmez , adj. Friendly; loving. muhabbat-i-dilī, yā qalbī . True love. muhabbat rakhnā , v. n. To have an affection for. muhabbat kā josh . The passion of love. Na khāne kī sudh aur na pīne kā hosh, Bharā dil meṅ uske muhabbat kā josh . Mīr Hasan. muhabbat kā dam bharnā , v. n. lit . to breathe of love. To profess attachment. muhabbat k. , v. a. To love; to be attached to. muhabbat kī nigāh, yā nazar ḍālnā , v. n. 1. To look kindly or sweet upon. 2. To cast love glances. matlab kī muhabbat . Cupboard love.
(orig. محببت from حبّ) s. f. Friendship, love, affection. محبت آميز muḥabbat-āmez, adj. Loving, friendly.
subs: or maḥabbat-ě-qalbī, s.f. Sincere affection, true love
To breathe (or talk of) love or affection, to profess attachment or friendship (for)
(-kī), To feel love or affection (for), to be attached (to)
(or nigāh) ḍālnā (-par), To cast loving glances (upon), to look kindly or sweetly (upon)
Love for an object, interested affection; cupboard love
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُحَبَّت (muhabbat), borrowed from Arabic مَحَبَّة (maḥabba), from حَبَّ (ḥabba). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi محبت (mḥbt).

def: s.m. See s.v. mahā. s.f. A cow.

s. m. 1. A grand epick poem by Vyāsadeva, containing an account of the dissensions and wars of the Kurus and Pāṇḍus, two great collateral branches of the house of Bhārat, so called from Bharat its founder. 2. The great war of the descendants of Bharat.

adj. & s.m. See s.v. mahaṭ. adj. & s.m. Great, large, big, bulky, huge; mighty, strong; great, important, eminent, high, noble, chief, excellent, illustrious, glorious; greatest, best, pre-eminent; — a great or eminent personage; a religious superior, a prior, an abbot; a chief of faqīrs; a monk (i.q. mahant); — greatness, magnitude; infinity; — importance, grandeur, dignity; pride, haughtiness, conceit, airs (in this sense, com. used by women, and pronounced maht, and also used as a fem.); — kingdom, dominion; — the great intellectual principle, the intellect
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Dependent upon, or attached to, the great; — one who is depenḍent on the great
(S. mahat+ojas), adj. Having great vigour or energy, very vigorous; full of vital power, very mighty or powerful; having great splendour or glory

def: s.m. = mahaushadh, q.v. s.m. A very efficacious remedy, a great or sovereign remedy, a panacea; — epithet of certain very strong or pungent plants, (such as) dried ginger; garlic, Allium ascalonicum; birch; long pepper.
s. m. A name of Siva, one of the principal Hindū divinities.
s.m. See s.v. mahā. s.f. A cow.

s. m. 1. A banker, money-dealer, merchant. 2. A good or trust-worthy person.
adj. Mercantile, commercial; of or relating to a banker; — s.f. Mercantile business, commerce, trade; — banking business, banking; exchange; interest; commission.
s. f. 1. The business of a mahājan. 2. Interest.
adj. Appertaining to great persons; fit for (or suitable to) great persons; fit for merchants.

fragrance; an act of fragrancing
wety: From مَہَک (mahak) or کار (kār). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi مہکار (mhkar).

adj. Fragrant, odoriferous; aromatic (i.q. mahkīlā, q.v.)
subs: or mahak uṭhnā, or mahak mārnā, v.n. = mahaknā, q.v.

r: mahak'nā, mahk ānā, mārnā yā uṭhnā
def: v. n. To exhale agreeable smells; to emit odour; to be fragrant.
more: v. n. To exhale agreeable smells, to emit odour, to perfume.

def: v.n. To exhale agreeable scent; to diffuse fragrance, to emit odour; to smell pleasant; to be fragrant
subs: v.n. To be scented or perfumed.

def: oblique plural of مَہَک (mahak, “fragrance; scent; aroma”)
vocative plural of مَہَک (mahak, “fragrance; scent; aroma”)

misspelling of محل
adj. Formidable, dreadful (place).
(noun of place from هول Heb. ) adj. Formidable, dreadful (places).

s.m. (pl. of maḥall, q.v.), Places; houses; streets; squares; courts; wards; districts; departments; — land or public fund producing a revenue to the government; real or landed estate; — a bee-hive, a honey-comb (pl. maḥallāt).
more: n. m. 1. Real or landed estate. 2. A honeycomb ( kukṛī ). Ek mandir ke sattar dar, Har dar meṅ tiryā kā ghar, Bīch meṅ vāke imrat tāl: Būjh hai uskī baṛī muhāl . Riddle. Ans. mahāl . Honey-comb. A house with seventy doors and rooms, In each a female bright there blooms, A fount of nectar 'tween them springs; This riddle's hard 'mong puzzling things.

s. m. plur. (of محل or محله from حلّ) Streets, squares, courts, places, houses, districts, departments. ¶ muḥāl (part. pass. IV. of حول) adj. Impossible, absurd (plur. محالات muḥālāt ). محال مطلق muḥāl-i-mut̤laḳ, Very difficult, absolutely impossible, quite absurd.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُحَال (muhāl), borrowed from Arabic مُحَال (muḥāl), from أَحَالَ (ʔaḥāla). First attested in c. 1657 as Middle Hindi محال (mḥal /⁠muhāl⁠/).

ety: n. of place fr. حلّ 'to alight, or descend, &c. (in a place
def: s.m. Place (in general; but orig. 'place of alighting, or of abiding'), position, situation; abode, residence, house, building, mansion, palace; hall, or chamber (of a grandee's residence); a seraglio; — post, station; district, quarter; — (met.) the wife of a noble; consort of a king, a queen (from the fact of each queen occupying a separate palace); — time; occasion, opportunity, opportune moment
more: n. m. 1. Place; situation; position. 2. A palace. Jiskī mahal meṅ maiyā, māṅge paisā mile rupaiyā . Prov. Whose mother in a palace hath command for pice Rupees come to his hand. 3. A queen, from each queen occupying a separate palace; a royal lady. Har bādshāh ke pāṅch chhaë mahal hote haiṅ. mahl-i-khās . The head wife ( patṭrānī ). mahl-dār , n. m. The watchman of a division. mahl-dārnī , n. f. 1. The midwife of a mahal or division. 2. The head of a brothel; the mother bawd. 3. The female superintendent of a lock hospital. mahal-sarā, mahal sarāë, mahal . A seraglio; female apartments. bar-mahal , adj. Appropriate; fitting. be-mahal , adj. Inappropriate; out of place.

adj. Fine; not coarse. Mīhīṅ kātūṅ, mīhīṅ pīsūṅ mīhīṅ sīm dikhāūṅ, Phir bhī sāsū kahe na chātar, ke bis khā mar jāūṅ? Rus. Though fine I spin, and fine I grind, and needle fine I ply, My mother-in-law ne'er praises me, I'll poison take and die. mahīn āvāz , 1. ( Mus .) A soprano voice. 2. A thin feeble voice. Sar kāṅpā, chāṅdī bāl hue, mūṅh pīlā, palkeṅ ān jhukīṅ, Qad ṭeṛhā, kān hue bahre, aur āṅkheṅ bhī chuṅ- dhiāë gaīṅ, Sukh nīṅd gaī, aur bhūk ghaṭī, dil sust huā, āvāz mahīṅ, Jo honī thī so ho guzrī, ab chalne meṅ kuchh der nahīṅ, Tan sūkhā, kubṛī pīṭ huī, ghoṛe par zīn dharo, bābā! Ab maut-naqārā bāj chukā, chalne kī fikr karo, bābā! Old age. Nazīr. Your head now shakes, your hair is white, pale-faced, your eyelids droop, Your cars are deaf, your eyes are dim, your body hath a stoop, Sound sleep and spirits fled, your hunger's less, your voice has grown so thin, What was to be has happened now, your march must soon begin, Shrunk is your frame, your back is bowed, your horse now saddle, friend! The drum of death hath beaten been, of marching think, my friend! mahīn gunḍā , Hypocritical ( siqa-bad-maāsh ).
more: adj. 1. Fine, subtile, thin (not coarse). 2. Great, greatest, elder born. مـﮩين دوز mihīn-doz, A tailor, a person who performs any fine needlework.
